findings.rationale: The image element has no alt attribute, no aria-label, no aria-labelledby, and no computed accessible name. It is not marked decorative and is not inside a functional context (link/button), making it an informative image that must have a text alternative. The absence of any accessible name violates SC 1.1.1.

findings.selector: body > div:nth-of-type(4) > div:nth-of-type(2) > div:nth-of-type(2) > div > div > div > div > section:nth-of-type(3) > div > div:nth-of-type(1) > div > figure > img
findings.rationale: The image is a real, rendered heart icon that clearly conveys meaningful information (a heart symbol, likely representing passion, love, or a brand identity for 'herzblut'). However, it is marked as decorative with alt="" and no other accessible name source. This is a coding inconsistency: the image is informative but treated as decorative, leaving screen reader users without access to its meaning. SC 1.1.1 requires a text alternative for non-text content; an empty alt attribute is only appropriate for genuinely ornamental images.

findings.rationale: The element is categorized as INFORMATIVE (not decorative, not functional). The accessible name is 'Sprechblase_s', which is a filename rather than a meaningful description of the image content. The screenshot shows a real image—a purple speech bubble with a white heart icon—but the alt text does not describe what the image depicts; it merely repeats the filename. A proper description would be something like 'Heart speech bubble' or 'Speech bubble with heart icon'. This fails SC 1.1.1 because the alt text does not provide an adequate text alternative that conveys the image's informative purpose.

findings.rationale: The image is a decorative speech bubble with a heart icon, which clearly conveys visual information and is not purely ornamental. Although marked with alt="" (decorative pattern), the image is not inside a link or button, making it an informative element. An informative image with an empty accessible name fails SC 1.1.1 because it provides no text alternative to describe its content.

findings.rationale: The image is a decorative speech bubble icon (rendered correctly in the screenshot), but its accessible name is 'Sprechblase_s'—a raw filename rather than a meaningful description. For an informative image, the alt text should describe what the image depicts (e.g., 'speech bubble' or 'comment icon'), not expose the filename. This fails SC 1.1.1 because the accessible name does not adequately convey the image's purpose or content.

findings.selector: body > div:nth-of-type(4) > div:nth-of-type(2) > div:nth-of-type(2) > div > div > div > div > section:nth-of-type(15) > div > div:nth-of-type(1) > div > figure > img
findings.rationale: The image is a portrait photograph of a person (real image rendered successfully) that conveys informative content, but it has an empty alt attribute and no other accessible name. Although marked as decorative via alt="", the image clearly depicts meaningful content (a portrait) that is not purely ornamental. An empty alt on an informative image fails SC 1.1.1.

The surrounding context from the ARIA snapshot confirms this is part of a slideshow control interface with related links like 'start slideshow' and 'jump to slide' options, further supporting that the purpose is clear.

evidence.surroundingContext: Über uns Wir sind eine Below the Line Agentur, die seit vielen Jahren durch persönliche Betreuung und Umsetzungsstärke besticht. Wir gehen jedes Projekt strukturiert, kreativ und mit viel Herzblut an. Unsere Philosophie: Jede Marke bekommt die passende Idee. Auch aus kleinen Budgets können tolle Projekte entstehen. Persönliche Betreuung schafft Vertrauen und so perfekte Ergebnisse. Wir leben unser

findings.outerHTML: • HERZBLUT KISTERL

evidence.textContent: HERZBLUT KISTERL Weihnachtskisterl Winzerkisterl

findings.rationale: The text consists entirely of proper names and German words. 'HERZBLUT KISTERL' appears to be a registered product or brand name (Kisterl is a Bavarian/Austrian term for a small box or basket, commonly used in wine/gift product naming). 'Weihnachtskisterl' (Christmas basket) and 'Winzerkisterl' (vintner's basket) are German compound nouns descriptive of product variants. All tokens are either German or part of a German-language product naming convention; no substantive foreign-language content requiring annotation is present.

findings.outerHTML: • Portfolio

evidence.textContent: Portfolio Promotion Sponsoring Events Staffing

findings.rationale: The text consists of four English words: 'Portfolio', 'Promotion', 'Sponsoring', 'Events', 'Staffing'. These are common English loanwords and technical/business terms that have become part of the vernacular in German business and marketing contexts. Each word is a single term without substantive phrases or clauses. Under SC 3.1.2 normative exceptions, loanwords and technical terms with no widely-used German equivalent are exempt from annotation requirements. No annotation is required.
